questions.question: What three decisions does a voice agent make on every 20ms chunk?
options: Is this frame speech (VAD), has the user started a new utterance (onset detection), and has the user finished (end-pointing / turn-end)
questions.explanation: Get either of the big two wrong and the assistant either cuts users off or never shuts up. Naive energy thresholds fail on traffic, keyboards, and crowd babble.

questions.question: What is the three-tier VAD cascade?
options: Tier 1: cheap energy gate (RMS threshold ~-40 dBFS); tier 2: Silero VAD (1M params, 6000+ languages, ~1ms per chunk, 87.7% TPR at 5% FPR); tier 3: a semantic turn detector that distinguishes "pause mid-sentence" from "done talking" using linguistic context
questions.explanation: Each tier filters for the next: energy removes obvious silence cheaply, Silero removes noise, and the turn detector handles the genuinely hard linguistic judgment of turn-completion.

questions.question: What does the pre-roll buffer fix?
options: Keeping 300-500ms of audio from BEFORE the VAD fires prevents the user's first word ("hey") from being clipped — VAD detection inherently lags speech onset
questions.explanation: Pairs with the other defaults: minimum speech 250ms rejects coughs, silence hangover 500-800ms avoids cutting off mid-sentence pauses, and a 0.3 threshold trades false positives for fewer first-word clips.

questions.question: How does the Kyutai flush trick cut end-of-turn latency?
options: Streaming STT has a look-ahead delay (500ms for Kyutai STT-1B); when VAD fires end-of-speech, a flush signal forces immediate STT output — since STT processes ~4x realtime, the 500ms buffer drains in ~125ms
It lowers the audio sample rate
questions.explanation: Without the flush you'd wait the full look-ahead after the user stops. With it: ~125ms VAD + flush = conversational latency.

questions.question: How do the 2026 VAD options compare?
options: WebRTC VAD (2013) manages only 50% TPR at 5% FPR; Silero hits 87.7% at ~1ms and is the open default; Cobra (commercial) reaches 98.9%; energy-only VAD has no place in 2026 production
Only commercial VADs run in real time
questions.explanation: Silero is the right default; Cobra is the accuracy/compliance upgrade. The TPR-at-fixed-FPR framing matters because false positives wake the whole expensive pipeline.
